candidateSelector.description: CSS selector limiting which nodes are evaluated. Wider = catches more, slower. Narrow it as you learn which selectors are real positives.

id.description: Must match a key in src/core/detectors/index.js

minScore.description: Sum of (weight * confidence) across matched detectors must reach this to be considered a modal. Definitive detectors bypass this.

selectors.description: Nodes matching any of these selectors are never removed, even if detectors flag them.

autoAccept.description: Browser-only feature: instead of removing the cookie modal, click the site's own Accept button so the site handles its own close (releasing scroll lock, persisting consent, etc.). Activated by passing { autoAccept: true } to removeModals() in the browser API. Has no effect on server-side HTTP cleaning (no JS execution there).

buttonSelectors.description: Per-vendor CSS selectors for the Accept-all button. Tried in order; all matches are clicked.

declineSelectors.description: Per-vendor CSS selectors for the Reject/Decline button. Used when preferences.cookies = 'decline'. The site's own decline handler runs (records 'rejected' state, releases scroll lock).

waitAfterClickMs.description: Milliseconds to wait after clicking to let the site's close animation run before evaluating what (if anything) is left to manually remove. Default 300.

textFallback.description: Fallback strategy when no vendor selector matches: scan visible button text inside cookie/consent containers and click any whose text matches one of the configured patterns. Includes deny-word safety to avoid clicking 'Reject all', 'Manage preferences', etc.

scopeSelector.description: CSS selector for containers within which buttons are eligible. Restricts text matching to cookie/consent banners only — prevents accidentally clicking 'Accept terms' on checkout pages.

maxClicks.description: Max number of buttons to click per pass. Default 1 — most pages need only one Accept click.

patterns.description: Case-insensitive button text patterns to match (textContent, value attr, aria-label). Exact-match preferred; falls back to short-haystack contains-match.

denyWords.description: Words that, if present in button text, exclude the button from matching. Catches 'Reject all', 'Manage settings', 'Necessary only', etc.

cleanup.description: Runs after main removal IF at least one modal was removed. Restores scroll on html/body and sweeps orphan backdrop/wrapper nodes that survive as siblings of the removed modal.

targets.description: CSS selector for the nodes to unlock. Default: 'html, body'.

removeClasses.description: Class names to strip from target nodes (e.g. 'modal-open', 'no-scroll').

removeStyleProperties.description: Inline style properties to strip from target nodes. Each item is either a property name string (always stripped if present) OR an object with conditions: { prop, ifValue?, ifSiblingProp? }. Conditional form lets you strip 'position' only when it equals 'fixed' (iOS scroll-lock signature) rather than always — avoids breaking sites with legitimate 'body { position: relative }'.

prop.description: The CSS property name to strip.

ifValue.description: Only strip if the property currently equals this value (e.g. 'fixed' for position).

ifSiblingProp.description: Only strip if all of these other properties on the SAME node match the given values (snapshotted before stripping begins). Used e.g. to strip 'top' only when 'position: fixed' is also set.

selectors.description: CSS selectors for orphan backdrop/wrapper nodes to sweep (e.g. '.modal-backdrop').

injectScrollUnlock.description: Inject a <style> tag with !important overrides as a last-resort scroll restorer. Catches the case where overflow:hidden lives in a <style> block or external stylesheet (not in class/inline attributes that we can strip). true = inject default CSS; false = skip; object = use { css } as the override.
